title: Why Berlin's Streets Are So Wide - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin was not always this open:** the historic centre around Alexanderplatz and St. Mary's Church used to be much denser.
**War damage changed the scale:** after 1945, large parts of Berlin were rubble, with **600,000 apartments destroyed**.
**The Marienviertel street plan vanished:** an official Berlin-Mitte panel says GDR planners completely eliminated the medieval street plan in the late 1960s.
**Karl-Marx-Allee explains the feeling:** the former Stalinallee was widened to **90 metres** and designed for state ceremony and parades.
**The wide streets are evidence:** old Berlin, wartime destruction, Nazi planning shadows, GDR ambition and reunified Berlin all overlap in the streetscape.
